author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm, belonging to the number of the Songs of Degrees, teacheth us, while we ascend and raise our minds unto the Lord our God in loving charity and piety, not to fix our gaze upon men who are prosperous in this world, with a happiness that is false and unstable, and altogether seductive; where they cherish nothing save pride, and their heart freezeth up against God, and is made hard against the shower of His grace, so that it beareth not fruit.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They that put their trust in the Lord shall be even as the mount Sion: they shall not be removed for ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are these?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall stand fast for ever, who dwell in Jerusalem” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we understand this earthly Jerusalem, all who dwelt therein have been excluded by wars and by the destruction of the city: thou now seekest a Jew in the city of Jerusalem, and findest him not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then will “they that dwell in Jerusalem not be moved for ever,” save because there is another Jerusalem, of which ye are wont to hear much?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She is our mother, for whom we sigh and groan in this pilgrimage, that we may return unto her.…They then who dwell therein “shall never be moved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who dwelt in that earthly Jerusalem, have been moved; first in heart, afterwards by exile.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they were moved in heart and fell, then they crucified the King of the heavenly Jerusalem herself; they were already spiritually without, and shut out of doors their very King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they cast Him out without their city, and crucified Him without.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He too cast them out of His city, that is, of the everlasting Jerusalem, the Mother of us all, who is in Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this Jerusalem?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He briefly describes it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The mountains stand around Jerusalem” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it anything great, that we are in a city surrounded by mountains?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this the whole of our happiness, that we shall have a city which mountains surround?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we not know what mountains are? or what are mountains save swellings of the earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Different then from these are those mountains that we love, lofty mountains, preachers of truth, whether Angels, or Apostles, or Prophets.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They stand around Jerusalem; they surround her, and, as it were, form a wall for her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of these lovely and delightful mountains Scripture constantly speaketh.…They are the mountains of whom we sing: “I lifted up mine eyes unto the mountains, from whence my help shall come:” because in this life we have help from the holy Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And through the mountains that receive peace, the little hills received righteousness: for what saith he of the mountains themselves?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, they have peace from themselves, or they make peace, or generate peace; but, they receive peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord is the source, whence they receive peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So therefore lift up thine eyes to the mountains for the sake of peace, that thy help may come from the Lord, who hath made heaven and earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, the Holy Spirit mentioning these mountains saith this: “Thou dost light them wonderfully from Thy everlasting mountains.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, the mountains light them: but, Thou lightest them from Thy everlasting mountains: through those mountains whom Thou hast willed to be everlasting, preaching the Gospel, Thou lighting them, not the mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such then are the “mountains that stand around Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that ye may know what sort of mountains these be that stand around Jerusalem; where Scripture hath mentioned good mountains, very rarely, and hardly, and perhaps never, doth it fail instantly to mention the Lord also, or allude to Him at the same moment, that our hopes rest not in the mountains.…Lest thou again shouldest tarry in the mountains, he at once addeth, “Even so the Lord standeth round about His people:” that thy hope might not lie in the mountains, but in Him who lighteth the mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when He dwelleth in the mountains, that is, in the Saints, He Himself is round about His people; and He hath Himself walled His people with a spiritual fortification, that it may not be moved for evermore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when Scripture speaketh of evil mountains, it addeth not the Lord unto them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such mountains, we have already told you often, signify certain mighty, but evil, souls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ye are not to suppose, brethren, that heresies could be produced through any little souls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: None save great men have been the authors of heresies; but in proportion as they were mighty, so were they evil, mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were not such mountains as would receive peace, that the hills might receive righteousness; but they received dissension from their father the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There were therefore mountains: beware thou fly not to such mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For men will come, and say unto thee, There is a great hero, there is a great man!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great was that Donatus!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great is Maximian! and a certain Photinus, what a great man he was!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Arius too, how illustrious he was!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these I have mentioned are mountains, but mountains that cause shipwreck.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But love such mountains, in whom the Lord is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then do those very mountains love thee, if thou hast not placed hope in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See, brethren, what the mountains of God are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence they are so called in another passage: “Thy righteousness is like the mountains of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not their righteousness, but “Thy righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear that great mountain the Apostle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That I may be found in Him, not having mine own righteousness, which is of the law, but that which is through the faith of Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who have chosen to be mountains through their own righteousness, as certain Jews or Pharisees their rulers, are thus blamed: “Being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and going about to establish their own righteousness, they have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who have submitted themselves are exalted in such a manner as to be humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that they are great, they are mountains; in that they submit themselves unto God, they are valleys: and in that they have the capacity of piety, they receive the plenteousness of peace, and transmit the copious irrigation to the hills, only beware, at present, what mountains thou lovest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou wish to be loved by good mountains, place not thy trust even in good mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how great a mountain was Paul? where is one like him found?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We speak of the greatness of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can any one readily be found of so great grace?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, he feared lest that bird should place trust in him: and what doth he say: “Was Paul crucified for you?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But lift up your eyes unto the mountains, whence help may come unto you: for, “I have planted, Apollos hath watered:” but, your help cometh from the Lord, who hath made Heaven and earth; for, “God gave the increase.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The mountains,” therefore, “stand around Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as “the mountains stand around Jerusalem, even so standeth the Lord round about His people, from this time forth for evermore.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If therefore the mountains stand around Jerusalem, and the Lord standeth round about His people, the Lord bindeth His people into one bond of love and peace, so that they who trust in the Lord, like the mount Sion, may not be moved for evermore: and this is, “from this time forth for evermore.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord will not leave the rod of the ungodly upon the lot of the righteous, lest the righteous put forth their hands unto wickedness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At present indeed the righteous suffer in some measure, and at present the unrighteous sometimes tyrannize over the righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what ways?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes the unrighteous arrive at worldly honours: when they have arrived at them, and have been made either judges or kings; for God doth this for the discipline of His folk, for the discipline of His people; the honour due to their power must needs be shown them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus hath God ordained His Church, that every power ordained in the world may have honour, and sometimes from those who are better than those in power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the sake of illustration I take one instance; hence calculate the grades of all powers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The primary and every day relation of authority between man and man is that between master and slave.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Almost all houses have a power of this sort.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are masters, there are also slaves; these are different names, but men and men are equal names.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what saith the Apostle, teaching that slaves are subject to their masters?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Servants, be obedient to them that are your masters according to the flesh:” for there is a Master according to the Spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is the true and everlasting Master; but those temporal masters are for a time only.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou walkest in the way, when thou livest in this life, Christ doth not wish to make thee proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It hath been thy lot to become a Christian, and to have a man for thy master: thou wast not made a Christian, that thou mightest disdain to be a servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when by Christ’s command thou servest a man, thou servest not the man, but Him who commanded thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith this also: “Servants, be obedient to them that are your masters according to the flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, he hath not made men free from being servants, but good servants from bad servants.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much do the rich owe to Christ, who orders their house for them! so that if thou hast had an unbelieving servant, suppose Christ convert him, and say not to him, Leave thy master, thou hast now known Him who is thy true Master: he perhaps is ungodly and unjust, thou art now faithful and righteous: it is unworthy that a righteous and faithful man should serve an unjust and unbelieving master.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He spoke not thus unto him, but rather, Serve him: and to confirm the servant, added, Serve as I served; I before thee served the unjust.…If the Lord of heaven and earth, through whom all things were created, served the unworthy, asked mercy for His furious persecutors, and, as it were, showed Himself as their Physician at His Advent (for physicians also, better both in art and health, serve the sick): how much more ought not a man to disdain, with his whole mind, and his whole good will, with his whole love to serve even a bad master!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, a better serveth an inferior, but for a season.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand what I have said of the master and slave, to be true also of powers and kings, of all the exalted stations of this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sometimes they are good powers, and fear God; sometimes they fear not God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Julian was an infidel Emperor, an apostate, a wicked man, an idolater; Christian soldiers served an infidel Emperor; when they came to the cause of Christ, they acknowledged Him only who was in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he called upon them at any time to worship idols, to offer incense; they preferred God to him: but whenever he commanded them to deploy into line, to march against this or that nation, they at once obeyed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They distinguished their everlasting from their temporal master; and yet they were, for the sake of their everlasting Master, submissive to their temporal master.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But will it be thus always, that the ungodly have power over the righteous?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It will not be so.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The rod of the ungodly is felt for a season upon the lot of the righteous; but it is not left there, it will not be there for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A time will come, when Christ, appearing in his glory, shall gather all nations before Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And thou wilt see there many slaves among the sheep, and many masters among the goats; and again many masters among the sheep, many slaves among the goats.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all slaves are not good—do not infer this from the consolation we have given to servants—nor are all masters evil, because we have thus repressed the pride of masters.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are good masters who believe, and there are evil: there are good servants who believe, and there are evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as long as good servants serve evil masters, let them endure for a season.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God will not leave the rod of the ungodly upon the lot of the righteous.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why will He not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lest the righteous put forth their hand unto wickedness:” that the righteous may endure for a season the domination of the ungodly, and may understand that this is not for ever, but may prepare themselves to possess their everlasting heritage.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he therefore addeth, “Do well, O Lord, unto those that are good and true of heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who are right in heart, of whom I was speaking a little before,—they who follow the will of God, not their own will,—reflect upon this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they who wish to follow God, allow Him to go before, and themselves to follow; not themselves to go before, and Him to follow; and in all things they find Him good, whether chastening, or consoling, or exercising, or crowning, or cleansing, or enlightening; as the Apostle saith, “We know that all things work together for good to them that love God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence the Psalmist at once addeth: “As for such as turn aside, the Lord shall lead them forth unto strangling with the workers of unrighteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5): that is, those whose deeds they have imitated; because they took delight in their present pleasures, and did not believe in their punishments to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then shall they have, who are righteous in heart, and who turn not back?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us now come to the heritage itself, brethren, for we are sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall we possess?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is our heritage? what is our country: what is it called?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this we salute you, this we announce to you, this the mountains receive, and the little hills receive as righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peace is Christ: “for He is our peace, who hath made both one, and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since we are sons, we shall have an inheritance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what shall this inheritance be called, but peace?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And consider that they who love not peace are disinherited.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now they who divide unity, love not peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peace is the possession of the pious, the possession of heirs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who are heirs?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sons.…Since then Christ the Son of God is peace, He therefore came to gather together His own, and to separate them from the wicked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From what wicked men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From those who hate Jerusalem, who hate peace, who wish to tear unity asunder, who believe not peace, who preach a false peace to the people, and have it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom answer is made, when they say, “Peace be with you,” “And with thy spirit:” but they speak falsely, and they hear falsely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto whom do they say, Peace be with you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To those whom they separate from the peace of the whole earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And unto whom is it said, “And with thy spirit”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To those who embrace dissensions, and who hate peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if peace were in their spirit, would they not love unity, and leave dissensions?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Speaking then false words, they hear false words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us speak true words, and hear true words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us be Israel, and let us embrace peace; for Jerusalem is a vision of peace, and we are Israel, “and peace is upon Israel.”
